title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY BOLILA, MUNICIPALITY OF MALITA, PROVINCE OF DAVAO OCCIDENTAL TO BE KNOWN AS THE BOLILA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
Daily Tribune, June 20, 2023; 119 OG No. 26, 4764 (June 26, 2023)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 11945, June 15, 2023 ]

AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY BOLILA, MUNICIPALITY OF MALITA, PROVINCE OF DAVAO OCCIDENTAL TO BE KNOWN AS THE BOLILA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

There is hereby established, under the supervision of the Department of Education, a national high school in Barangay Bolila, Municipality of Malita, Province of Davao Occidental to be known as the Bolila National High School.

SEC. 2. The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the Department's program the operationalization of the Bolila National High School, the funding of which shall be included in the annual Genera Appropriations Act.

SEC. 3. Within ninety (90) days from the approval of this Act, the Secretary of Education shall formulate the rules and regulations implementing the provisions of this Act. Such implementing rules and regulations shall provide for the school's organization, administration, operations and management, in accordance with Republic act No. 9155, or the "Governance of Basic Education Act of 2001," Republic Act No. 10533, or the "Enhanced Basic Education Act of 2013," and other basic education-related laws.

SEC. 4. This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette

or in a newspaper of general circulation.

Approved,

(SGD.)

JUAN MIGUEL F. ZUBIRI

President of the Senate

(SGD.)

FERDINAND MARTIN G. ROMUALDEZ

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act, which originated in the House of Representatives, was passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate of the Philippines on January 31, 2023 and March 20, 2023, respectively

.

(SGD.)

RENATO N. BANTUG, JR.

Secretary of the Senate

(SGD.)

REGINALD S. VELASCO

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved: 15 JUN 2023

(SGD.)

FERDINAND ROMUALDEZ MARCOS JR.

President of the Philippines
